prompt: Our tests use AnyIO and `pytest-asyncio`, and CI got flaky right after someone set both plugins to auto mode. We also have a module-scoped async fixture for a TCP listener, and we want to run the important tests on Trio, plain asyncio, and asyncio+uvloop. Show the right pytest setup and tell me what not to do.
assertions: It explicitly mentions the AnyIO vs `pytest-asyncio` auto-mode conflict.
It provides a concrete `anyio_backend` fixture or equivalent configuration.
It includes Trio and plain asyncio in the serious portability matrix.
It treats `asyncio+uvloop` as an additional runtime axis rather than a substitute for Trio coverage.
It explicitly states that a higher-scope async fixture needs a matching higher-scope `anyio_backend` fixture.
It prefers free-port fixtures or listener-discovered ports over hardcoded ports.
